title: SkillMeta
description: Schema for meta.json — the machine-readable metadata file required at the root of every skill.

name.description: Unique skill identifier. Must be kebab-case, lowercase, alphanumeric and hyphens only. Must match the skill's directory name exactly.

version.description: Semantic version of the skill. New skills must start at 1.0.0.

description.description: One sentence describing what the skill produces. Must start with a verb. Maximum 120 characters.
examples: Converts a Figma component description into production-ready Tailwind CSS markup.
Generates a conventional commit message from a git diff.

trigger.description: 2–3 sentences telling Claude when to activate this skill. Must include at least one boundary condition (when NOT to activate).
examples: Activate this skill when the user provides a Figma design description or asks to implement a UI component. Do not activate for generic CSS questions unrelated to a Figma source.

domain.description: The single domain this skill belongs to.

tags.description: Between 2 and 8 lowercase kebab-case tags. Must be specific — avoid generic terms like tool, helper, utility.

author.description: GitHub handle of the skill author, without the @ prefix.

model_compatibility.description: Models this skill has been tested and confirmed to work with. List only models you have personally verified.

requires_tools.description: Claude Code tools this skill requires to function. Can be empty if no tools are needed.

license.description: SPDX license identifier. Use MIT for most skills, CC-BY-4.0 for content-only skills.
